summary: A relief rally swept global markets today after a two-week US-Iran ceasefire deal was struck. WTI crude tumbled as much as 19% after Trump agreed to suspend bombing of Iran to restore Strait of Hormuz flows, with Brent sliding 14% to around $94 per barrel. Fed rate cut expectations have evaporated, with an extended hold now the base case, leaving geopolitics as the dominant driver across all assets.
tweet: Iran ceasefire deal sends oil crashing 19% today, sparking a broad relief rally — but the Fed is on hold and gold's drop flags that safe-haven demand is already unwinding fast.
